signals.raw:

signals.headline: Market-moving implications:**
signals.raw:
Market-moving implications:**

The key confirmation is that **rate cuts are off the table**—the BoK remains focused on managing upside inflation risks from surging global oil costs and currency weakness (won at ~1,520 per USD).[4] The Policy Board expects inflation to trend toward the "mid to upper two-percent range" this year.[4] This signals the easing cycle has ended and rates will remain at 2.

signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rate at 2.5% Amid 2.2% Inflation | NO (to rate cuts) | HIGH confidence**

As of **April 10, 2026**, the Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board unanimously decided to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.5% for the 7th consecutive month.[4] South Korea's annual inflation rose to **2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months but below market forecasts
